story.hook: The moon shows us one face for a reason.

story.description: A clip of connected habitats, biodiversity, food webs, or ecosystem interactions: underwater coral reef with vibrant marine life
story.hook: Coral shows why the reef city matters.
story.lead: Coral reefs feed the ocean. See that cleaner wrasse darting through? It eats parasites off fish that might otherwise go blind or die. That's why big predators like groupers don't eat them—they're tiny doctors. Coral waste doesn't just sit there; it feeds algae that feeds the whole reef.

story.description: A close clip of insects: burning incense stick with rising smoke
story.hook: Ants leave invisible roads for the colony
story.lead: Ants wave their antennae in smoke—that's how they sniff out danger. They don't follow the scent trail, they feel it in the air currents with tiny hairs on their feelers. That's why they can reroute the whole line in seconds. Did you ever see them do that?
